calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Successor Trustee'S Bond
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Christine Witherspoon'S Final Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-07-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Jordan Goldstein; Maureen McFadden for Christine Witherspoon.

Ms. McFadden reported that she has sent emails and text messages to Christine Witherspoon to get the information needed to prepare the accounting, but has received no response. Mr. Schultz stated that it probably didn't make sense to wait any longer for a response from Ms. Witherspoon, as Ms. Goldstein could prepare a forensic accounting. He said that there should probably be a surchare against Ms. Witherspoon as trustee, but that it wasn't clear at this point if there has been malfeasance or merely nonfeasance on her part. It was the opinion of Mr. Schultz that the status hearing should go off calendar at this time. The Status Hearing re filing of Christine Witherspoon's Final Accounting was ordered off calendar by the Court.

Judge: Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Successor Trustee'S Bond
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Continued To Mar-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Christine Witherspoon And For Appointment Of Jordan Goldstein As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for David Brown (Petitioner).
Andrew Schultz for and with Jordan Goldstein (temporary Trustee/proposed Successor Trustee).
Maureen McFadden for Christine Witherspoon (suspended Trustee).

Ms. McFadden stated that she has not heard from Ms. Witherspoon, and that she has no objection to the Court ordering removal of Ms. Witherspoon for failure to meet her duties and appointing a Successor. Mr. Schultz stated that there are two Schwab accounts with a combined total of $761,000.00, some Bank of America accounts that seem to be in the Estate and other accounts to be determined. Mr. Schultz also proposed an initial bond of $850,000.00, stated that there is no evidence of taking but some wasting, that some distributions were made but paid back, that principal was invaded with some penalties, and that the taxes on the residence are current. Furthermore, Mr. Schultz stated that taxes on the second property are not paid and that there are penalties, and that they have not investigated into the income taxes. Ms. Callejo stated that her Client is in support. The Court granted the Petition for Removal of Trustee Christine Witherspoon and for Appointment of Jordan Goldstein as Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $850,000.00. The Court will not order Court supervision at this time. Ms. Witherspoon is to turn over all Trust assets and records. Status on filing of Ms. Witherspoon's final accounting is set for March 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition For Suspension Of Trustee Christine Witherspoon And For Appointment Of Jordan Goldstein As Temporary Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
